Habit number seven.

Habit number four.
Warm miso broth with wilted cabbage and
fresh ginger.
This stuff is criminally underrated.
Your gut lining is a barrier, one cell
thick, that separates digested food from
your bloodstream. When that lining is
damaged, waste particles leak through
and trigger inflammation throughout your
entire body.
Miso is a fermented soybean paste packed
with live probiotics, specifically
lactobacillus and bifidobacterium
strains that directly repair and
repopulate that damaged gut lining. A
study in the journal Beneficial Microbes
found daily miso consumption improved
intestinal barrier function by 34%.
34%. People report reduced bloating
within 48 hours. When you wilt napa
cabbage into warm broth, you're adding
glutamine, the single most important
amino acid for colon cell repair.
Fresh ginger warms the core, relaxing
the smooth muscle of your intestinal
walls.
Three ingredients, one bowl.
A tub of miso paste costs about $4 and
lasts 3 weeks. A head of cabbage is $2.
Fresh ginger may be a dollar, roughly 30
cents per serving. One
oz of warm, not boiling water.
Boiling kills the probiotics. Stir in

Grate in a half inch of fresh ginger.
Drink it first thing in the morning. One

Habit number three,
warm water with rice vinegar and raw

But for colon motility specifically,

of.
When rice vinegar hits your stomach, it
triggers the gastrocolic reflex, a
direct nerve signal from your stomach to
your colon that says, "Start moving."
Your stomach fills, the acid level
shifts, and your colon receives a

Then the acetic acid feeds
acetate-producing bacteria in your lower

Those bacteria produce short-chain fatty

Drink it 20 minutes before breakfast.

Habit number two,
hara hachi bu with roasted barley tea.
This is the single most powerful daily

Hara hachi bu is the Okinawan practice
of eating until you're only 80% full.
You stop before you feel stuffed.
Pound for pound, this habit destroys

When you chronically overeat, your

Waste backs up. Peristalsis can't keep

Pair this with mugicha, traditional

Start each meal by placing 20% less food
on your plate.
Eat slowly.
When you feel the first whisper of

stop.
Sip warm barley tea between bites to

Habit number one, timed warm water
windows with lemon and sea salt.

You drink specific amounts of warm water

Warm water converts thermal energy into

Fresh lemon juice introduces citric acid

A pinch of unrefined sea salt creates an

Your windows.
First, 6:00 to 7:00 a.m., 16 oz of warm

Drink slowly over 15 minutes on a

This ignites your gastrocolic reflex

8 oz of warm water with a squeeze of

8 oz of plain warm water, sipped slowly.
This keeps the colon hydrated overnight.
